title: rubus tanakae var. manazurensis
intro: Rubus tanakae var. manazurensis, also known as Japanese black raspberry, is a variant of the Rubus tanakae plant species, a type of black raspberry native to Japan. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. The roots and stems of the plant are commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, Rubus tanakae var. manazurensis is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
